full_text:
**[QC batch 2]{.underline}**

Use the "napari fast masking" jupyter notebook with a scimap
environment, maybe some new packages need to be installed, so try with
some time ahead

- CHANNELS are in channels_tif2

Napari script opens images automatically, click file\>open file\> +
select your mask (either cell or nucleus is fine)

1. Click in the polygon icon to create a shapes layer

2. Draw boundaries of the tissue to remove falsely masked cells

3. Clear down the background channels.

4. For rest of the channels, go to the excel in
 > F:\\farkkilab2\\4_CellCycle\\tcycif\\4_10_Expansion\\Data\\qc\\max_thresholds,
 > and state the [minimum pixel values of the antibody
 > aggregates]{.underline}. Do not remove aggregates in these
 > channels manually.

 a. To verify this is fine, please double check with DNA channel
 > that the signals do not overlap with nuclei

5. Clear down all artifacts related to hairs, fibers etc

6. Detachment is evaluated by DNA7 vs DNA0, and only big detached areas
 > (\<10% of whole tissue) are removed, the rest is automatically
 > cleared with a script later on

7. Once it is ready, save your shapes tab by clicking over it and then
 > file\>save selected layer. Outputs should be saved with sample
 > name in :
 > F:\\farkkilab2\\4_CellCycle\\tcycif\\4_10_Expansion\\Data\\qc\\annotations

king over it and then
 > file\>save selected layer. Outputs should be saved with sample
 > name in :
 > F:\\farkkilab2\\4_CellCycle\\tcycif\\4_10_Expansion\\Data\\qc\\annotations

Once done marked progress in 2 Computational steps tab of project 4_10
experimental file.

**TRY NOT TO USE SQUARE/CIRCLE TOOLS, FREEHAND SELECTION LOOKS MORE
NATURAL**
